title: Jack Gets in the Game

jokes.text: This is a $54 steak... And of course I can't eat it because of my recent, uh... heart attack. Secret heart attack.
jokes.setup: Jack proudly presents an expensive steak as if it's a gift
jokes.punchline: He can't eat it due to his secret heart condition and wants to watch Liz eat it
jokes.explanation: Perfectly character-specific corporate executive behavior - buying expensive things he can't enjoy and making subordinates do uncomfortable things. The 'secret heart attack' detail adds absurdist specificity.

jokes.text: You want to watch me eat this steak in front of you? That's what I want.
jokes.setup: Normal person's reaction to bizarre request
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact confirmation of the weird power play
jokes.explanation: The directness and weird corporate dominance is peak Jack behavior. Economy is perfect - no wasted words.

jokes.text: Uh, no. I subscribe to Giant Boats.
jokes.setup: Jack asks about reading 'Yachting Illustrated'
jokes.punchline: Liz's blue-collar alternative magazine name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's both a pun and character moment. 'Giant Boats' is absurdly literal and perfectly captures Liz's anti-pretentious attitude. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: 'like business, with a pillowy abyss of a lover's bosom, seems infinite'... Pillowy abyss? Ignore that part.
jokes.setup: Jack reading pretentious business quote from magazine
jokes.punchline: His embarrassment at the sexual metaphor and instruction to ignore it
jokes.explanation: The absurdity of mixing business wisdom with sexual imagery, plus Jack's corporate discomfort with anything inappropriate. 'Pillowy abyss' is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: By talking about sex in a sailing magazine? That's exactly how Margaret Thatcher did it.
jokes.setup: Liz questioning the business succession signals
jokes.punchline: Jack's completely false historical reference delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Jack's false corporate wisdom delivered with complete authority. The Margaret Thatcher reference is so specific and wrong it's brilliant.

jokes.text: You ate that whole thing? A dog took it. He came out of nowhere.
jokes.setup: Jack's surprise at the empty plate
jokes.punchline: Liz's absurd excuse delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: The deadpan delivery of an obviously made-up excuse. Economy is perfect - the specificity of 'he came out of nowhere' sells it.

jokes.text: Oh, it's the Japanese porn star diet. I only eat paper, but I can eat all the paper I want, so...
jokes.setup: Question about Jenna's crash diet
jokes.punchline: Absurdly named diet with impossible premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - falling for ridiculous celebrity diet trends. The 'all the paper I want' clause makes it even more absurd.

jokes.text: No...no, no, no-- you are fat. Now go and see Dr. Spaceman right now and get this taken care of.
jokes.setup: Liz giving supportive body-positive speech to Jenna
jokes.punchline: Jack's blunt contradiction and corporate solution approach
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack cutting through PC nonsense with corporate efficiency. The contrast with Liz's speech makes it effective.

jokes.text: Like James Gandolfini or Fat Albert?
jokes.setup: Jenna discussing double standards for heavy men
jokes.punchline: Comparing a real actor to a cartoon character as equivalent examples
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's shallow understanding of cultural issues. The cartoon/real person equivalence works.

jokes.text: What, are we back in college freshman year? Let's go to the common room and talk about apartheid.
jokes.setup: Liz making earnest points about body image standards
jokes.punchline: Jack's dismissive comparison to naive college activism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack cynicism about earnest liberal causes. The specificity of 'apartheid' and 'common room' makes it feel authentic.

jokes.text: I'm not making excuses, Jack. But this is taken care of-- ow! Oh, it hurts! I missed a dentist appointment this morning.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming she has her life together
jokes.punchline: Immediate physical evidence contradicting her claim
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic timing - her tooth hurts right as she denies having problems. Good structure with immediate payoff.

jokes.text: You drawn to the phallic nature of our skyline?
jokes.setup: Greeting an old colleague visiting the city
jokes.punchline: Absurdly sexual interpretation of NYC architecture
jokes.explanation: Typical Jack making everything vaguely sexual and intellectual. Good economy in the greeting format.

jokes.text: You can't, you're gay. No, not anymore, friend.
jokes.setup: Jack's disbelief about Devin's engagement
jokes.punchline: Devin's matter-of-fact claim to have changed sexual orientation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the absurd cult storyline. Economy is excellent - delivers the premise quickly.

jokes.text: You mean the cult that was invented by Stan Lee? The religion founded by the alien king living inside Stan Lee.
jokes.setup: Reference to 'Church of Practicology'
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd details about the fake religion
jokes.explanation: Good Scientology parody with creative Stan Lee twist. The escalation from cult to alien king works well.

jokes.text: I can totally feel the gay draining right out of me. By the eye of Zolnak, right, guys?
jokes.setup: Devin explaining his conversion from gay to straight
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous description plus fake alien god reference
jokes.explanation: The physical description of orientation 'draining' plus the alien oath makes it appropriately ridiculous.

jokes.text: ICU81MI 'I see you ate one, am I?' Hilarious.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his vanity license plate that Angie called inscrutable
jokes.punchline: The actual meaning is grammatically nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy - thinks he's clever but the actual meaning makes no sense. The license plate format is creative and the self-congratulation sells it.

jokes.text: Angie is in the past, like Dracula and broadcast television.
jokes.setup: Tracy dismissing his relationship with Angie
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison grouping vampire with media format
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy non-sequitur comparison. The grouping of completely unrelated 'outdated' things is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: With who? That guy Mike that re-did our driveway?
jokes.setup: Kenneth suggesting Tracy would be jealous if Angie found someone new
jokes.punchline: Tracy's immediate specificity about who it would be
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy has obviously thought about this already. The specific 'driveway guy' detail makes it feel real and jealous.

jokes.text: For your height, your weight puts you in what we call the disgusting range.
jokes.setup: Medical consultation about Jenna's weight
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate medical terminology delivered professionally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman - completely unprofessional advice delivered with medical authority. 'Disgusting range' is memorably awful.

jokes.text: Crystal meth has been shown to be very effective. How important is tooth retention to you?
jokes.setup: Asking about weight loss solutions
jokes.punchline: Suggesting illegal drugs with casual medical follow-up question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from unprofessional to illegal, with the tooth question showing he knows the side effects but still recommends it.

jokes.text: Oh, please-- Dr. Spaceman is my dad. Call me Leo.
jokes.setup: Jenna addressing him formally as 'Dr. Spaceman'
jokes.punchline: Casual correction implying his father is also a doctor with the same ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: The implication that there are multiple generations of Dr. Spacemans adds to the absurdity. Good economy and character building.

jokes.text: That's where the Olsen twins were separated.
jokes.setup: Mentioning the Bradshaw clinic for surgery
jokes.punchline: Implying the twins were conjoined and surgically separated there
jokes.explanation: Dark and unexpected twist on celebrity twins. Perfect economy - one line completely recontextualizes the Olsen twins.

jokes.text: I can't be on television looking like I just had a baby or something.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her desperation for surgery
jokes.punchline: Comparing weight gain to post-pregnancy appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's vanity but not particularly original or structured.

jokes.text: You know, this right here is why I got into medicine.
jokes.setup: After recommending dangerous surgery to boost someone's TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Claiming this shallow vanity project represents his medical calling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman moment - completely backwards medical priorities. Character integration is perfect.

jokes.text: Alan Garkel in legal-- I don't think he really needs that wheelchair.
jokes.setup: Discussing how everyone has secrets at work
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting coworker is faking disability
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate humor about disability fraud. Good economy and character-appropriate cynicism.

jokes.text: Mr. Banks, Mr. Donaghy eats guys like you as part of a healthy breakfast.
jokes.setup: Kenneth defending Jack against Devin's threats
jokes.punchline: Innocent Kenneth using aggressive metaphor in cereal commercial format
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - trying to be tough but using wholesome breakfast metaphor. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Heart attack? Really? Right, Mr. D? High five! Not now? Okay.
jokes.setup: Kenneth accidentally revealing Jack's secret heart attack
jokes.punchline: His oblivious attempt to get validation and celebration
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth moment - completely missing social cues while being enthusiastically unhelpful. The high-five request is perfectly tone-deaf.

jokes.text: Hey, I've got a character for Jenna called, 'Me want food.' She's in a supermarket and she keeps going, 'Me want food!'
jokes.setup: Writers' room discussion about Jenna's new weight
jokes.punchline: Frank's incredibly insensitive and dumb sketch idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank - completely inappropriate and unfunny. The repetition and caveman speak makes it appropriately awful.

jokes.text: Fat Hillary. She can be, like, 'Me want food!'
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting Jenna play Hillary Clinton
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately adapting it to his awful catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Frank's one-track mind. Less original since it's repeating the same joke.

jokes.text: At least I don't live with my mom--ow.
jokes.setup: Liz defending herself against Jack's criticism
jokes.punchline: Tooth pain interrupting her comeback attempt
jokes.explanation: Good callback timing but fairly predictable. Economy works well with the interruption.

jokes.text: Holy crap, did your tooth just fall out? Oh, that's not great, huh?
jokes.setup: Liz's ongoing dental problems escalating
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual understatement of obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag. Frank's casual reaction works for the character.

jokes.text: Boys becoming men, men becoming wolves
jokes.setup: Tracy showing off his gold record
jokes.punchline: Absurd bar mitzvah/werewolf song lyrics
jokes.explanation: Absolute genius moment. Combining bar mitzvah and werewolf themes is completely unexpected and perfect. Became a cultural phenomenon and perfectly captures Tracy's absurd celebrity projects.

jokes.text: Hello, Angie. I hear you're single now. That's cool. What? I like your top. I'm a real good sex person. I do it all the different ways.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to seduce Angie per Tracy's instructions
jokes.punchline: Painfully innocent attempt at being sexually aggressive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - trying to be seductive but sounding like a robot learning about sex. 'I'm a real good sex person' is hilariously awkward phrasing.

jokes.text: Why not? I have a grandkid who calls me Don. Next time he does it, I'm marching that little bastard to a recruiting station.
jokes.setup: Devin asking if he can call him 'dad'
jokes.punchline: Geiss's violent reaction to casual address from family
jokes.explanation: Great character introduction - shows Geiss is even more intense than Jack. The escalation from grandkid to military service is perfect.

jokes.text: She got big.
jokes.setup: Seeing Kathy Geiss for the first time
jokes.punchline: Jack's blunt assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good Jack directness but not particularly creative. Economy is excellent though.

jokes.text: Me want food!
jokes.setup: Kathy speaking for the first time
jokes.punchline: She actually talks exactly like Frank's offensive sketch idea
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that makes Frank's awful idea accidentally prophetic. The timing and setup make it work perfectly.

jokes.text: That's not what I hear.
jokes.setup: Jack saying he couldn't eat much meat
jokes.punchline: Devon's sexual innuendo while pretending to be straight
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre showing Devon can't help being inappropriate

jokes.text: Get the old heart rate up. Maybe pound a cheeseburger in the sun, throw some butter on it. You're gonna love it! I'm gonna make your heart explode.
jokes.setup: Devin describing the party activities to Jack
jokes.punchline: Escalating from healthy activities to explicitly trying to kill Jack
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from seemingly innocent party to explicit murder attempt. The progression feels natural while being completely insane.

jokes.text: I got something on my mind grapes I need to talk to you about.
jokes.setup: Tracy wanting to discuss something serious
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical replacement of 'mind' with 'mind grapes'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy malapropism. 'Mind grapes' is perfectly absurd and became a fan favorite quote. Character integration is perfect.

jokes.text: I want you to go to my house and make love to my wife, Ken. Jiminy Christmas!
jokes.setup: Tracy's plan to make things fair with Angie
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate request followed by Kenneth's innocent exclamation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pairing of Tracy's insane logic with Kenneth's wholesome shock. Both characters are perfectly served and the premise is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: 'Cause we live on Dacrib Avenue.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his GPS nickname for home
jokes.punchline: The address actually justifies the nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that makes Tracy's nickname actually make sense, which is funnier than if it were random.

jokes.text: Hey!Oh!Me want food! This woman was a mouseketeer, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Random person yelling the catchphrase at Jenna
jokes.punchline: Frank defending her dignity while she's being mocked
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the catchphrase has entered public consciousness

jokes.text: Hey, I am wanting the foods! Very good. Very nice.
jokes.setup: Random person recognizing Jenna
jokes.punchline: Using the 'me want food' catchphrase with broken English
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how the catchphrase has spread. The broken English variation adds something new.

jokes.text: You earned me a grand! Thank you.
jokes.setup: Jack helping Devin in the wrestling match
jokes.punchline: Jack bet against himself and made money from his own failure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack moment - turning his own humiliation into profit. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Your back is like a barrel of snakes! Oh, god! I'm just your doll! I think I love you. We're joking! It's all jokes!
jokes.setup: Jack wrestling to prove fitness
jokes.punchline: Increasingly homoerotic commentary followed by desperate denial
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Jack's desperation leading to inadvertent homoerotic moment

jokes.text: Your back is like a barrel of snakes! Oh, god! I'm just your doll! I think I love you. We're joking! It's all jokes!
jokes.setup: Wrestling match getting uncomfortably homoerotic
jokes.punchline: Devin's over-the-top expressions followed by desperate backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy showing Devin's 'straightness' is completely fake. The escalation and panic are expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Did you know, scientifically speaking, that humans want food but don't need it.
jokes.setup: Doctor giving medical opinion
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of human biology
jokes.explanation: Peak Dr. Spaceman - authoritative delivery of completely wrong medical information

jokes.text: I'm keeping it! The fat. I've decided to keep it because people recognize me, and I get off on it.
jokes.setup: Jenna at the surgery clinic about to get the procedure
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal - she likes the attention from being heavy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - any attention is good attention. The complete reversal of her motivation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And who's wearing a one-piece swimsuit instead of underwear. I have to do laundry.
jokes.setup: Jenna criticizing Liz's life choices
jokes.punchline: Liz's pathetic excuse for inappropriate undergarments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz - completely inappropriate life choices justified with weak excuses. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Just so you know, if you hear this sound at your funeral, it's just me laughing!
jokes.setup: Making heart attack sound effects to taunt Jack
jokes.punchline: Promising to laugh at Jack's funeral
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Devin's villainy. The specificity of promising to laugh at the funeral is appropriately dark.

jokes.text: This boy comes to the door, tries to kiss me, then he throws up and starts crying.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's attempt to seduce Angie per Tracy's plan
jokes.punchline: Complete failure described in escalating humiliation
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Kenneth's earlier awkward attempt. The escalation from kiss to vomit to crying is perfectly Kenneth.

jokes.text: No more jewelry with my name misspelled. 'Anjy'
jokes.setup: Angie listing Tracy's thoughtless romantic gestures
jokes.punchline: Visual of misspelled jewelry showing his carelessness
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Tracy's thoughtlessness. The misspelling is specific and believable.

jokes.text: This grilled cheese has mayonnaise in it! What?
jokes.setup: Angie demanding Tracy stay with her constantly
jokes.punchline: Her immediate outrage at food preparation detail
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Angie's controlling nature will extend to everything. Economy works well.

jokes.text: I mean, I almost let a man die today, Caitlin. And for what? For a bigger office? For more money?
jokes.setup: Jack having apparent moral crisis about his ambition
jokes.punchline: The things he's questioning are exactly what he wants
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but fairly standard corporate conscience crisis. The self-awareness works.

jokes.text: Get to know my secret family up in Canada.
jokes.setup: Geiss describing his retirement plans
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning secret family like a normal retirement activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geiss moment - treating scandalous secret like mundane retirement plan. Economy is excellent and it's highly quotable.

jokes.text: You mind if I watch you eat that? Okay.
jokes.setup: End of episode with Jack and Liz back to normal dynamic
jokes.punchline: Return to the opening's weird power dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect structural callback that closes the episode's loop. Shows nothing has really changed in their dynamic.

tmdb_overview: When Jack discovers that his boss is retiring, he decides to get in the competition for his position. Meanwhile, Jenna begins to enjoy her fame for being fat and along with Jack encourages Liz to get her act together. Finally, Kenneth makes it his mission to get Tracy and Angie back together.
insight: Elite density at 2.92 jokes per minute sustains 91-point humor through character chaos.
